prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a nearly black screen, with no discernible objects or text visible.

By the final sampled frame, the image has changed to a graphic end-card style layout on a dark green/black background. Near the top center is a bordered rectangular picture showing a brown/orange Chevrolet truck or SUV viewed from the front-left, driving outdoors under a blue sky. Below the picture, large white text reads “CHEVY” on one line and “TOUGH” beneath it. A small emblem or badge at the lower right contains the word “TRUCKS,” though the smaller surrounding text is not fully clear. A thin orange horizontal line runs across the lower-left area, just above or beside the text.

No people are visible in the sampled frames. The content between the black opening frame and the final branded truck graphic is not visible from the provided samples.
